revision.comment: 新しいページ：大伴武日。
revision.text:
'''大伴 武日'''（おおとも の たけひ{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}、生没年不詳）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「大伴武日連（おおとものたけひのむらじ）」、他文献では「'''大伴健日連'''」とも表記される。『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。 [[大伴氏|大伴連（大伴氏）]]の遠祖である。[[垂仁天皇]]朝では五大夫の1人に数えられるほか、[[景行天皇]]朝における[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]東征の従者の1人である。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]25年2月8日条では、[[武渟川別]]（[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣]]祖）・[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・大鹿島（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・十千根（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）らとともに「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[酒折宮]]（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]）
{{Smaller|『日本書紀』に見える「酒折宮」伝承地。}}]] また同書[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によれば、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征に際して、[[吉備武彦]]とともに従者に任じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。東征では、[[甲斐国|甲斐]]の酒折宮（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]酒折に比定）において日本武尊から靭部（ゆげいのとものお）を賜ったという{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）11月11日条では、[[伴善男]]の奏言のうちで、健日は景行天皇の時に倭武命（日本武尊）に従って東国を平定し、その功で[[讃岐国]]を賜ったと見える{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。またその奏言では、子の[[大伴武以|大伴健持（武以/武持）]]を始めとして子孫の名が記載されるが、その中で[[允恭天皇]]朝に倭胡連公が[[讃岐国造]]に任じられたという{{Sfn|大伴武日（国史）}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[直木孝次郎]]|year=|chapter=大伴武日|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大伴武日（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大伴連武日|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[大伴氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の たけひ}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:大伴氏]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人おおとものたけひ]]

'''大伴 武日'''（おおとも の たけひ{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}、生没年不詳）は、『[[日本書紀]]』等に伝わる古代[[日本]]の[[人物]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。 『[[日本書紀]]』では「大伴武日連（おおとものたけひのむらじ）」、他文献では「'''大伴健日連'''」とも表記される。『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。 [[大伴氏|大伴連（大伴氏）]]の遠祖である。[[垂仁天皇]]朝では五大夫の1人に数えられるほか、[[景行天皇]]朝における[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]東征の従者の1人である。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]25年2月8日条では、[[武渟川別]]（[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣]]祖）・[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・大鹿島（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・十千根（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）らとともに「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[酒折宮]]（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]）
{{Smaller|『日本書紀』に見える「酒折宮」伝承地。}}]] また同書[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によれば、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征に際して、[[吉備武彦]]とともに従者に任じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。東征では、[[甲斐国|甲斐]]の酒折宮（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]酒折に比定）において日本武尊から靭部（ゆげいのとものお）を賜ったという{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）11月11日条では、[[伴善男]]の奏言のうちで、健日は景行天皇の時に倭武命（日本武尊）に従って東国を平定し、その功で[[讃岐国]]を賜ったと見える{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。またその奏言では、子の[[大伴武以|大伴健持（武以/武持）]]を始めとして子孫の名が記載されるが、その中で[[允恭天皇]]朝に倭胡連公が[[讃岐国造]]に任じられたという{{Sfn|大伴武日（国史）}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[直木孝次郎]]|year=|chapter=大伴武日|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大伴武日（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大伴連武日|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[大伴氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の たけひ}} [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:大伴氏|たけひ]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人おおとものたけひ]]

{{Smaller|『日本書紀』に見える「酒折宮」伝承地。}}]] また同書[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によれば、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征に際して、[[吉備武彦]]とともに従者に任じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。東征では、[[甲斐国|甲斐]]の酒折宮（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]酒折に比定）において日本武尊から靭部（ゆげいのとものお）を賜ったという{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）11月11日条では、[[伴善男]]の奏言のうちで、大伴健日（武日）は景行天皇の時に倭武命（日本武尊）に従って東国を平定し、その功で[[讃岐国]]を賜ったと見える{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。またその奏言では、子の[[大伴武以|大伴健持（武以/武持）]]を始めとして子孫の名が記載されるが、その中で[[允恭天皇]]朝には倭胡連公が[[讃岐国造]]に任じられたとある{{Sfn|大伴武日（国史）}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[直木孝次郎]]|year=|chapter=大伴武日|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大伴武日（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大伴連武日|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[大伴氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の たけひ}} [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:大伴氏|たけひ]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|従おおとものたけひ]]

{{Smaller|『日本書紀』に見える「酒折宮」伝承地。}}]] また同書[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によれば、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征に際して、[[吉備武彦]]と共に従者に任じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。東征では、[[甲斐国|甲斐]]の酒折宮（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]酒折に比定）において日本武尊から靭部（ゆげいのとものお）を賜ったという{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）11月11日条では、[[伴善男]]の奏言のうちで、大伴健日（武日）は景行天皇の時に倭武命（日本武尊）に従って東国を平定し、その功で[[讃岐国]]を賜ったと見える{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。またその奏言では、子・[[大伴武以|健持（武以/武持）]]を始めとして子孫の名が記載されるが、その中で[[允恭天皇]]朝には倭胡連公が[[讃岐国造]]に任じられたとある{{Sfn|大伴武日（国史）}}。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[直木孝次郎]]|year=|chapter=大伴武日|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大伴武日（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大伴連武日|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} == 関連項目 == * [[大伴氏]] {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の たけひ}} [[Category:大伴氏|たけひ]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|従おおとものたけひ]]

{{Smaller|『日本書紀』に見える「酒折宮」伝承地。}}]] また同書[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によれば、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征に際して、[[吉備武彦]]と共に従者に任じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。東征では、[[甲斐国|甲斐]]の酒折宮（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]酒折に比定）において日本武尊から靭部（ゆげいのとものお）を賜ったという{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）11月11日条では、[[伴善男]]の奏言のうちで、大伴健日連は景行天皇の時に倭武命（日本武尊）に従って東国を平定し、その功で[[讃岐国]]を賜ったと見える{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。またその奏言では、子・[[大伴武以|健持（武以/武持）]]を始めとして子孫の名が記載されるが、その中で[[允恭天皇]]朝には倭胡連公が[[讃岐国造]]に任じられたとある{{Sfn|大伴武日（国史）}}。しかし大伴健日連が讃岐国を賜った事実はなく、讃岐の佐伯直とは無関係である「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/saeki-sa1.htm 讃岐の佐伯直とその一族]」『古樹紀之房間』2005年。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[直木孝次郎]]|year=|chapter=大伴武日|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大伴武日（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大伴連武日|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の たけひ}} [[Category:大伴氏|たけひ]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人おおとものたけひ]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 =大伴武日 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 武日連、健日連、健日命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[垂仁天皇]]、[[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[豊日命]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = [[大伴武以|武以連]] | 特記事項 = }} '''大伴武日'''（おおとものたけひ{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}、生没年不詳）は、『[[日本書紀]]』などに伝わる[[古代日本]]の人物。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。 == 概要 == 『[[日本書紀]]』では「大伴武日連（おおとものたけひのむらじ）」、他文献では'''大伴健日連'''、'''健日命'''とも表記される。『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。 [[大伴氏|大伴連（大伴氏）]]の遠祖である。[[垂仁天皇]]朝では五大夫の1人に数えられるほか、[[景行天皇]]朝における[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]東征の従者の一人である。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]25年2月8日条では、[[武渟川別]]（[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣]]祖）・[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・[[国摩大鹿島命|大鹿島]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）らと共に「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[酒折宮]]（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]）
{{Smaller|『日本書紀』に見える「酒折宮」伝承地。}}]] また同書[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によれば、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征に際して、[[吉備武彦]]と共に従者に任じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。東征では、[[甲斐国|甲斐]]の酒折宮（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]酒折に比定）において日本武尊から靭部（ゆげいのとものお）を賜ったという{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）11月11日条では、[[伴善男]]の奏言のうちで、大伴健日連は景行天皇の時に倭武命（日本武尊）に従って東国を平定し、その功で[[讃岐国]]を賜ったと見える{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。またその奏言では、子・[[大伴武以|健持（武以/武持）]]を始めとして子孫の名が記載されるが、その中で[[允恭天皇]]朝には倭胡連公が[[讃岐国造]]に任じられたとある{{Sfn|大伴武日（国史）}}。しかし大伴健日連が讃岐国を賜った事実はなく、讃岐の佐伯直とは無関係である「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/saeki-sa1.htm 讃岐の佐伯直とその一族]」『古樹紀之房間』2005年。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[直木孝次郎]]|year=|chapter=大伴武日|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大伴武日（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大伴連武日|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の たけひ}} [[Category:大伴氏|たけひ]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人おおとものたけひ]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 =大伴武日 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 武日連、健日連、健日命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[垂仁天皇]]、[[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏]] | 父母 = 父：[[豊日命]] | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = [[大伴武以|武以連]] | 特記事項 = }} '''大伴武日'''（おおとものたけひ{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}、生没年不詳）は、『[[日本書紀]]』などに伝わる[[古代日本]]の人物。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。(大伴)[[豊日命]]の子。 == 概要 == 『[[日本書紀]]』では「大伴武日連（おおとものたけひのむらじ）」、他文献では'''大伴健日連'''、'''健日命'''とも表記される。『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。 [[大伴氏|大伴連（大伴氏）]]の遠祖である。[[垂仁天皇]]朝では五大夫の1人に数えられるほか、[[景行天皇]]朝における[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]東征の従者の一人である。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]25年2月8日条では、[[武渟川別]]（[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣]]祖）・[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・[[国摩大鹿島命|大鹿島]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）らと共に「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[酒折宮]]（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]）
{{Smaller|『日本書紀』に見える「酒折宮」伝承地。}}]] また同書[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によれば、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征に際して、[[吉備武彦]]と共に従者に任じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。東征では、[[甲斐国|甲斐]]の酒折宮（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]酒折に比定）において日本武尊から靭部（ゆげいのとものお）を賜ったという{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）11月11日条では、[[伴善男]]の奏言のうちで、大伴健日連は景行天皇の時に倭武命（日本武尊）に従って東国を平定し、その功で[[讃岐国]]を賜ったと見える{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。またその奏言では、子・[[大伴武以|健持（武以/武持）]]を始めとして子孫の名が記載されるが、その中で[[允恭天皇]]朝には倭胡連公が[[讃岐国造]]に任じられたとある{{Sfn|大伴武日（国史）}}。しかし大伴健日連が讃岐国を賜った事実はなく、讃岐の佐伯直とは無関係である「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/saeki-sa1.htm 讃岐の佐伯直とその一族]」『古樹紀之房間』2005年。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[直木孝次郎]]|year=|chapter=大伴武日|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大伴武日（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大伴連武日|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の たけひ}} [[Category:大伴氏|たけひ]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人おおとものたけひ]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 =大伴武日 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 武日連、健日連、健日命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[垂仁天皇]]、[[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏|大伴連]] | 父母 = 父：[[豊日命]] | 兄弟 = 乎多氐命 | 妻 = | 子 = [[大伴武以|武以連]]、蚊手連、阿古連 | 特記事項 = }} '''大伴武日'''（おおとものたけひ{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』などに伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[人物]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。[[崇神天皇|崇神朝]]の官人である[[豊日命]]の子。 == 概要 == 『[[日本書紀]]』では「大伴武日連（おおとものたけひのむらじ）」、他文献では'''大伴健日連'''、'''健日命'''とも表記される。『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。 [[大伴氏|大伴連（大伴氏）]]の遠祖である。[[垂仁天皇]]朝では五大夫の1人に数えられるほか、[[景行天皇]]朝における[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]東征の従者の一人である。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]25年2月8日条では、[[武渟川別]]（[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣]]祖）・[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・[[国摩大鹿島命|大鹿島]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）らと共に「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[酒折宮]]（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]）
{{Smaller|『日本書紀』に見える「酒折宮」伝承地。}}]] また同書[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によれば、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征に際して、[[吉備武彦]]と共に従者に任じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。東征では、[[甲斐国|甲斐]]の酒折宮（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]酒折に比定）において日本武尊から靭部（ゆげいのとものお）を賜ったという{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）11月11日条では、[[伴善男]]の奏言のうちで、大伴健日連は景行天皇の時に倭武命（日本武尊）に従って東国を平定し、その功で[[讃岐国]]を賜ったと見える{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。またその奏言では、子・[[大伴武以|健持（武以/武持）]]を始めとして子孫の名が記載されるが、その中で[[允恭天皇]]朝には倭胡連公が[[讃岐国造]]に任じられたとある{{Sfn|大伴武日（国史）}}。しかし大伴健日連が讃岐国を賜った事実はなく、讃岐の佐伯直とは無関係である「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/saeki-sa1.htm 讃岐の佐伯直とその一族]」『古樹紀之房間』2005年。。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[直木孝次郎]]|year=|chapter=大伴武日|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大伴武日（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大伴連武日|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の たけひ}} [[Category:大伴氏|たけひ]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人おおとものたけひ]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

contributor.username: ja>学者気取りの先頭はここで終わり！

{{Smaller|『日本書紀』に見える「酒折宮」伝承地。}}]] また同書[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によれば、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征に際して、[[吉備武彦]]と共に従者に任じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。東征では、[[甲斐国|甲斐]]の酒折宮（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]酒折に比定）において日本武尊から靭部（ゆげいのとものお）を賜ったという{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）11月11日条では、[[伴善男]]の奏言のうちで、大伴健日連は景行天皇の時に倭武命（日本武尊）に従って東国を平定し、その功で[[讃岐国]]を賜ったと見える{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。またその奏言では、子・[[大伴武以|健持（武以/武持）]]を始めとして子孫の名が記載されるが、その中で[[允恭天皇]]朝には倭胡連公が[[讃岐国造]]に任じられたとある{{Sfn|大伴武日（国史）}}。しかし大伴健日連が讃岐国を賜った事実はなく、讃岐の佐伯直とは無関係である「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/saeki-sa1.htm 讃岐の佐伯直とその一族]」『古樹紀之房間』2005年。。 == 伝承 == [[岩手県]][[陸前高田市]]には、「武日長者伝承」が存在する。この伝説によれば、武日は[[日本武尊]]の東征に従って[[気仙郡]]に至り、当地で[[姉歯の松|朝日姫、夕日姫]]の2人の娘をもうけたという。高田町大石には武日長者の屋敷跡と伝わる場所がある。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[直木孝次郎]]|year=|chapter=大伴武日|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大伴武日（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大伴連武日|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の たけひ}} [[Category:大伴氏|たけひ]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人おおとものたけひ]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 =大伴武日 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 武日連、健日連、健日命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[垂仁天皇]]、[[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏|大伴連]] | 父母 = 父：[[豊日命]] | 兄弟 = [[乎多氐命]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[大伴武以|武以連]]、蚊手連、阿古連 | 特記事項 = }} '''大伴武日'''（おおとものたけひ{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』などに伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[人物]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。[[孝霊天皇|孝霊朝]]・[[開化天皇|開化朝]]の官人である[[豊日命]]の子。 == 概要 == 『[[日本書紀]]』では「大伴武日連（おおとものたけひのむらじ）」、他文献では'''大伴健日連'''、'''健日命'''とも表記される。『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。 [[大伴氏|大伴連（大伴氏）]]の遠祖である。[[垂仁天皇]]朝では五大夫の1人に数えられるほか、[[景行天皇]]朝における[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]東征の従者の一人である。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]25年2月8日条では、[[武渟川別]]（[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣]]祖）・[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・[[国摩大鹿島命|大鹿島]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）らと共に「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[酒折宮]]（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]）
{{Smaller|『日本書紀』に見える「酒折宮」伝承地。}}]] また同書[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によれば、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征に際して、[[吉備武彦]]と共に従者に任じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。東征では、[[甲斐国|甲斐]]の酒折宮（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]酒折に比定）において日本武尊から靭部（ゆげいのとものお）を賜ったという{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）11月11日条では、[[伴善男]]の奏言のうちで、大伴健日連は景行天皇の時に倭武命（日本武尊）に従って東国を平定し、その功で[[讃岐国]]を賜ったと見える{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。またその奏言では、子・[[大伴武以|健持（武以/武持）]]を始めとして子孫の名が記載されるが、その中で[[允恭天皇]]朝には倭胡連公が[[讃岐国造]]に任じられたとある{{Sfn|大伴武日（国史）}}。 == 伝承 == [[岩手県]][[陸前高田市]]には、「武日長者伝承」が存在する。この伝説によれば、武日は[[日本武尊]]の東征に従って[[気仙郡]]に至り、当地で[[姉歯の松|朝日姫、夕日姫]]の2人の娘をもうけたという。高田町大石には武日長者の屋敷跡と伝わる場所がある。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[直木孝次郎]]|year=|chapter=大伴武日|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大伴武日（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大伴連武日|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の たけひ}} [[Category:大伴氏|たけひ]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人おおとものたけひ]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 =大伴武日 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 武日連、健日連、健日命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[垂仁天皇]]、[[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏|大伴連]] | 父母 = 父：[[豊日命]] | 兄弟 = [[乎多氐命]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[大伴武以|武以連]]、蚊手連、阿古連 | 特記事項 = }} '''大伴武日'''（おおとものたけひ{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』などに伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[人物]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。[[孝霊天皇|孝霊朝]]・[[開化天皇|開化朝]]の官人である[[豊日命]]の子。 == 概要 == 『[[日本書紀]]』では「大伴武日連（おおとものたけひのむらじ）」、他文献では'''大伴健日連'''、'''健日命'''とも表記される。『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。 [[大伴氏|大伴連（大伴氏）]]の遠祖である。[[垂仁天皇]]朝では五大夫の1人に数えられるほか、[[景行天皇]]朝における[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]東征の従者の一人である。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]25年2月8日条では、[[武渟川別]]（[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣]]祖）・[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・[[国摩大鹿島命|大鹿島]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）らと共に「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[酒折宮]]（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]）
{{Smaller|『日本書紀』に見える「酒折宮」伝承地。}}]] また同書[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によれば、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征に際して、[[吉備武彦]]と共に従者に任じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。東征では、[[甲斐国|甲斐]]の酒折宮（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]酒折に比定）において日本武尊から靭部（ゆげいのとものお）を賜ったという{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）11月11日条では、[[伴善男]]の奏言のうちで、大伴健日連は景行天皇の時に倭武命（日本武尊）に従って東国を平定し、その功で[[讃岐国]]を賜ったと見える{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。またその奏言では、子・[[大伴武以|健持（武以/武持）]]を始めとして子孫の名が記載されるが、その中で[[允恭天皇]]朝には倭胡連公が[[讃岐国造]]に任じられたとある{{Sfn|大伴武日（国史）}}。しかし大伴健日連が讃岐国を賜った事実はなく、讃岐の佐伯直とは無関係であると指摘されている「[http://wwr2.ucom.ne.jp/hetoyc15/keijiban/saeki-sa1.htm 讃岐の佐伯直とその一族]」『古樹紀之房間』2005年。。 == 伝承 == [[岩手県]][[陸前高田市]]には、「武日長者伝承」が存在する。この伝説によれば、武日は[[日本武尊]]の東征に従って[[気仙郡]]に至り、当地で[[姉歯の松|朝日姫、夕日姫]]の2人の娘をもうけたという。高田町大石には武日長者の屋敷跡と伝わる場所がある。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=[[直木孝次郎]]|year=|chapter=大伴武日|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大伴武日（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大伴連武日|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の たけひ}} [[Category:大伴氏|たけひ]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人おおとものたけひ]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.comment: 「wikipedia:検証可能性」、「Wikipedia:独自研究は載せない」というWikipediaの方針に反しているため差し戻し。[[Special:Contributions/大判小判|大判小判]] ([[User talk:大判小判|会話]]) による ID:88415143 の版を[[H:RV|取り消し]]

{{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |Image= |Image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[Emperor Suinin]], [[Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]][[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]]

According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]]

Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]]

{{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |Image= |Image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[Emperor Suinin]], [[Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]]

Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:new pages]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]]

{{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |Image= |Image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[Emperor Suinin]], [[Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:unsorted completion categories]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]]

In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]]

A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:bump 1-27]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:properly attributed pages]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Otomo no Takehiko}][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:subst:MAINSPACE]][[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |Image= |Image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[Emperor Suinin]], [[Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] to [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:{{subst:PAGENAME}}}}[[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |Image= |Image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[Emperor Suinin]], [[Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient {{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |Image= |Image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[Emperor Suinin]], [[Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient revision.comment: removed [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]]; added [[Category:Completion tier 5]] using [[Help:Gadget-HotCat|HotCat]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |Image= |Image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[Emperor Suinin]], [[Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |Image= |Image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[Emperor Suinin]], [[Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |Image= |Image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[Emperor Suinin]], [[Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite {{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |Image= |Image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[Emperor Suinin]], [[Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[en:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Takehiko}}
