{{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[Emperor Suinin]], [[Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[en:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Takehiko}}

revision.comment: 21 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大伴武日 up to rev 90266249
revision.text:
{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 =大伴武日 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[古墳時代]] | 生誕 = 不明 | 死没 = 不明 | 改名 = | 別名 = 武日連、健日連、健日命 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = | 主君 = [[垂仁天皇]]、[[景行天皇]] | 氏族 = [[大伴氏|大伴連]] | 父母 = 父：[[豊日命]] | 兄弟 = [[乎多氐命]] | 妻 = | 子 = [[大伴武以|武以連]]、蚊手連、阿古連 | 特記事項 = }} '''大伴武日'''（おおとものたけひ{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}、[[生没年不詳]]）は、『[[日本書紀]]』などに伝わる[[古墳時代]]の[[人物]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[連]]。[[孝霊天皇|孝霊朝]]・[[開化天皇|開化朝]]の官人である[[豊日命]]の子。 == 概要 == 『[[日本書紀]]』では「大伴武日連（おおとものたけひのむらじ）」、他文献では'''大伴健日連'''、'''健日命'''とも表記される。『[[古事記]]』に記載はない。 [[大伴氏|大伴連（大伴氏）]]の遠祖である。[[垂仁天皇]]朝では五大夫の1人に数えられるほか、[[景行天皇]]朝における[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]東征の従者の一人である。 == 記録 == 『[[日本書紀]]』[[垂仁天皇]]25年2月8日条では、[[武渟川別]]（[[阿倍氏|阿倍臣]]祖）・[[彦国葺命|彦国葺]]（[[和珥氏|和珥臣]]祖）・[[国摩大鹿島命|大鹿島]]（[[中臣氏|中臣連]]祖）・[[物部十千根|十千根]]（[[物部氏|物部連]]祖）らと共に「大夫（まえつきみ）」の1人に数えられており、天皇から神祇祭祀のことを命じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[酒折宮]]（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]）
{{Smaller|『日本書紀』に見える「酒折宮」伝承地。}}]] また同書[[景行天皇]]40年7月16日条によれば、[[ヤマトタケル|日本武尊]]の東征に際して、[[吉備武彦]]と共に従者に任じられている{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。東征では、[[甲斐国|甲斐]]の酒折宮（[[山梨県]][[甲府市]]酒折に比定）において日本武尊から靭部（ゆげいのとものお）を賜ったという{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。 『[[日本三代実録]]』[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]3年（[[861年]]）11月11日条では、[[伴善男]]の奏言のうちで、大伴健日連は景行天皇の時に倭武命（日本武尊）に従って東国を平定し、その功で[[讃岐国]]を賜ったと見える{{Sfn|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}。またその奏言では、子・[[大伴武以|健持（武以/武持）]]を始めとして子孫の名が記載されるが、その中で[[允恭天皇]]朝には倭胡連公が[[讃岐国造]]に任じられたとある{{Sfn|大伴武日（国史）}}。 == 伝承 == [[岩手県]][[陸前高田市]]には、「武日長者伝承」が存在する。この伝説によれば、武日は[[日本武尊]]の東征に従って[[気仙郡]]に至り、当地で[[姉歯の松|朝日姫、夕日姫]]の2人の娘をもうけたという。高田町大石には武日長者の屋敷跡と伝わる場所がある。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=直木孝次郎|authorlink=直木孝次郎|year=|chapter=大伴武日|title=[[国史大辞典 (昭和時代)|国史大辞典]]|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大伴武日（国史）}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=大伴連武日|title=日本古代氏族人名辞典 普及版|publisher=[[吉川弘文館]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{Harvid|大伴連武日（古代氏族）|2010年}}}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおとも の たけひ}} [[Category:大伴氏|たけひ]] [[Category:日本神話の人物]] [[Category:古墳時代の人物]] [[Category:古代日本の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:ヤマトタケル伝説|人おおとものたけひ]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[Emperor Suinin]], [[Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Province people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Otomo no Takehiko]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[en:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Ōtomo no Takehi}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Otomo no Takehiko}}

In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[en:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]]

{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[en:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:大伴氏]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]]

In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Figures in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese figures]] [[Category:Ancient Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:大和国の人物]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]]

In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ōtomo clan|Takehi]] [[Category:Kofun period people]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]]

In the reign of [[Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Yamato Takeru legend|Person Ōtomo no Takehi]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] [[Category:Yamato Takeru Legend]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]]

In the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[Kōfu]], [[Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[Rikuzentakata]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:生没年不詳]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]]

{{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[:en:Kaika|Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[:en:Rikuzentakata|Rikuzentakata]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]]

Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[:en:Rikuzentakata|Rikuzentakata]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]]

A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[sn:E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] [[Category:paes with no wikidata]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[:en:Kaika|Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[:en:Rikuzentakata|Rikuzentakata]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[sn:E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year of Birth and Death unknown]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] [[Category:Pages with no Wikidata]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Takemochi|qid=Q17222817}}|father={{ill|Q109287923|qid=Q109287923}}|native_name=大伴武日}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20041216]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Takehi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[:en:Kaika|Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[:en:Rikuzentakata|Rikuzentakata]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Otomo no Takehiko]] [[sn:E5%A4%A7%E4%BC%B4%E6%AD%A6%E6%97%A5]] [[sn:tomo no Takehiko]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]]

{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Takemochi|qid=Q17222817}}|father={{ill|Q109287923|qid=Q109287923}}|native_name=大伴武日}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20041216]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Takehi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. [[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|qid=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Takemochi|qid=Q17222817}}|father={{ill|Q109287923|qid=Q109287923}}|native_name=大伴武日}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q20041216]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Takehi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[:en:Kaika|Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[:en:Rikuzentakata|Rikuzentakata]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] {{wikidata link|Q20041216}}

[[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Takemochi|WD=Q17222817}}|father={{ill|Q109287923|WD=Q109287923}}|native_name=大伴武日}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041216]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Takehi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. [[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Takemochi|WD=Q17222817}}|father={{ill|Q109287923|WD=Q109287923}}|native_name=大伴武日}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041216]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Takehi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[:en:Kaika|Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[:en:Rikuzentakata|Rikuzentakata]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] {{wikidata link|Q20041216}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] {{wikidata link|Q20041216}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Takemochi|WD=Q17222817}}|father={{ill|Q109287923|WD=Q109287923}}|native_name=大伴武日}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041216]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Takehi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] {{wikidata link|Q20041216}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Takemochi|WD=Q17222817}}|father={{ill|Q109287923|WD=Q109287923}}|native_name=大伴武日}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041216]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Takehi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[:en:Kaika|Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[:en:Rikuzentakata|Rikuzentakata]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] {{wikidata link|Q20041216}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]][[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] {{wikidata link|Q20041216}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
[[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] [[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] [[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Takemochi|WD=Q17222817}}|father={{ill|Q109287923|WD=Q109287923}}|native_name=大伴武日}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041216]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Takehi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord=[[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]] |Clan=[[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and [[:en:Kaika|Kaika]] periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the [[:en:Ōtomo clan|Ōtomo Muraji]] (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]], he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] during his eastern expedition under [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]]'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the [[:en:Wani clan|Wani no Omi]]), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ([[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]])
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] in the 40th year of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]]'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day [[:en:Kōfu|Kōfu]], [[:en:Yamanashi Prefecture|Yamanashi Prefecture]]).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of [[:en:Jōgan|Jōgan]] (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted [[:en:Sanuki Province|Sanuki Province]] as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under [[:en:Emperor Ingyō|Emperor Ingyō]].{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[:en:Rikuzentakata|Rikuzentakata]], [[:en:Iwate Prefecture|Iwate Prefecture]], there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] {{wikidata link|Q20041216}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]
