According to this story, Takehi accompanied [[:en:Yamato Takeru|Yamato Takeru]] to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] {{wikidata link|Q20041216}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
[[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] [[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] [[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Takemochi|WD=Q17222817}}|father={{ill|Q109287923|WD=Q109287923}}|native_name=大伴武日}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q20041216]] Wikidata name: Ōtomo no Takehi [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:23, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:51, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord={{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}} |Clan={{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo Muraji}} |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and {{ill|Kaika|en|Kaika|WD=Q14867060|lt=Kaika}} periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo Muraji}} (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}, he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} during his eastern expedition under {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani no Omi}}), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}})
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} in the 40th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province}} as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|Emperor Ingyō|WD=Q349258|lt=Emperor Ingyō}}.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[:en:Rikuzentakata|Rikuzentakata]], {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] {{wikidata link|Q20041216}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo Muraji}} (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}, he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} during his eastern expedition under {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin}}'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani no Omi}}), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}})
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} in the 40th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture}}).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan}} (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province}} as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|Emperor Ingyō|WD=Q349258|lt=Emperor Ingyō}}.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[:en:Rikuzentakata|Rikuzentakata]], {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture}}, there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru}} to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] {{wikidata link|Q20041216}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Otomo clan]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]]

A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] {{wikidata link|Q20041216}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Ōtomo clan (ancient Japan)]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:pages]]

[[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] [[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] [[ja:大伴武日]][[zh:大伴武日]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}|nationality={{ill|Japan|WD=Q17|1=Japan}}|children={{ill|Ōtomo no Takemochi|WD=Q17222817|1=Ōtomo no Takemochi}}|father={{ill|Q109287923|WD=Q109287923|1=Toyohi no Mikoto}}|native_name=大伴武日}} {{Infobox noble |Name=Ōtomo no Takehi |image= |image size= |Caption= |Era=[[Kofun period]] |Birth Date=Unknown |Death Date=Unknown |Other Names=Takehi Muraji, Kenhi Muraji, Kenhi no Mikoto |Posthumous Name= |Deity Name= |Religious Name= |Grave= |Court Rank= |Lord={{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}} |Clan={{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo Muraji|1=Ōtomo clan}} |Parents=Father: [[Toyohi no Mikoto]] |Siblings=[[Ōtade no Mikoto]] |Spouse= |Children=[[Ōtomo no Takei|Takei Muraji]], Kate Muraji, Ako Muraji |Notes= }} '''Ōtomo no Takehi''' (大伴武日, also written as '''Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji''' or '''Kenhi no Mikoto''') was a [[Kofun period]] figure mentioned in the [[Nihon Shoki]] and other historical records. He held the [[kabane]] (hereditary title) of [[Muraji]] and was the son of [[Toyohi no Mikoto]], an official in the [[Kōrei]] and {{ill|Kaika|en|Kaika|WD=Q14867060|lt=Kaika|1=Kaika}} periods. == Overview == In the [[Nihon Shoki]], he is referred to as "Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji" (大伴武日連) and is also mentioned in other texts as "Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji" or "Kenhi no Mikoto." He does not appear in the [[Kojiki]]. He is considered a distant ancestor of the {{ill|Ōtomo clan|en|Ōtomo clan|WD=Q1096662|lt=Ōtomo Muraji|1=Ōtomo clan}} (later Ōtomo clan). In the reign of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}, he was one of the five high officials and later became a retainer of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} during his eastern expedition under {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}. == Records == According to the [[Nihon Shoki]], in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|en|Emperor Suinin|WD=Q314850|lt=Emperor Suinin|1=Emperor Suinin}}'s reign (2nd month, 8th day), Takehi was one of the "great officials" (大夫, maetsukimi), alongside [[Takenukawawake]] (ancestor of the [[Abe clan|Abe no Omi]]), [[Hiko Kunifuki]] (ancestor of the {{ill|Wani clan|en|Wani clan|WD=Q11417873|lt=Wani no Omi|1=Wani clan}}), [[Kuni no Shima]] (ancestor of the [[Nakatomi clan|Nakatomi no Muraji]]), and [[Mononobe no Tokoneme]] (ancestor of the [[Mononobe clan|Mononobe no Muraji]]), and was entrusted with the duties of Shinto rituals.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} [[File:Sakaorimiya shaden.jpg|thumb|200px|right|[[Sakaori Shrine]] ({{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu|1=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}})
{{smaller|Traditionally identified as the "Sakaori Shrine" mentioned in the Nihon Shoki.}}]] The [[Nihon Shoki]] also describes his role during the eastern expedition of {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} in the 40th year of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}'s reign. Along with [[Kibi no Takehiko]], he served as a retainer and received a ceremonial quiver from Yamato Takeru at [[Sakaori Shrine]] (traditionally identified with a site in present-day {{ill|Kōfu|en|Kōfu|WD=Q201932|lt=Kōfu|1=Kōfu}}, {{ill|Yamanashi Prefecture|en|Yamanashi Prefecture|WD=Q132720|lt=Yamanashi Prefecture|1=Yamanashi Prefecture}}).{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}} In the [[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] entry for the 3rd year of {{ill|Jōgan|en|Jōgan|WD=Q1075635|lt=Jōgan|1=Jōgan}} (861), the retainer [[Bando Yoshio]] recounts that Ōtomo no Kenhi Muraji assisted Yamato Takeru in pacifying the eastern provinces and was granted {{ill|Sanuki Province|en|Sanuki Province|WD=Q929682|lt=Sanuki Province|1=Sanuki Province}} as a reward. This account also mentions his descendants, including [[Ōtomo no Takei|Kenmochi (Takei/Kentake)]], and states that one of his line became a [[Sanuki no Kuni no Miyatsuko|provincial governor]] under {{ill|Emperor Ingyō|en|Emperor Ingyō|WD=Q349258|lt=Emperor Ingyō|1=Emperor Ingyō}}.{{Sfn|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}} == Legends == In [[:en:Rikuzentakata|Rikuzentakata]], {{ill|Iwate Prefecture|en|Iwate Prefecture|WD=Q48326|lt=Iwate Prefecture|1=Iwate Prefecture}}, there is a local legend called the "Takehi Chōja Legend." According to this story, Takehi accompanied {{ill|Yamato Takeru|en|Yamato Takeru|WD=Q461258|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru}} to [[Kesen District]] during the eastern expedition and fathered two daughters, Morning Sun Princess and Evening Sun Princess. A location in Ōishi, Takada Town, is said to be the site of Takehi Chōja's residence. == Notes == {{Reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=Takashiro Naoki|authorlink=Takashiro Naoki|year=|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi|title=[[Kokushi Daijiten]]|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Historical Sources)}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2010|chapter=Ōtomo no Takehi Muraji|title=Japanese Ancient Clan Names Dictionary (Popular Edition)|publisher=[[Yoshikawa Kōbunkan]]|isbn=9784642014588|ref={{SfnRef|Ōtomo no Takehi (Ancient Clans)|2010}}}} {{translated page|ja|大伴武日|version=90266249|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:大伴武日]] [[zh:大伴武日]] {{wikidata link|Q20041216}} [[Category:Characters in Japanese mythology]] [[Category:Japanese warriors]] [[Category:Ōtomo clan (ancient Japan)]] [[Category:People of ancient Japan]] [[Category:People of Kofun-period Japan]] [[Category:People of Yamato Province]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Year of birth unknown]] [[Category:Year of death unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_birth_unknown]] [[Category:Year_of_death_unknown]] [[Category:pages]]
